text: Walk me through your resume.
Why investment banking?
Why this bank specifically?
Tell me about a deal or transaction you have been following in the news. What makes it interesting?
What do you think makes a successful investment banker?
Tell me about a time you worked on a team under extreme time pressure.
Investment banking requires long hours and significant personal sacrifice. How do you handle stress and maintain your performance?
Describe a time you had to pay close attention to detail to avoid a significant mistake.
Tell me about a time you had to manage multiple projects simultaneously. How did you prioritize?
What other firms are you interviewing with? Where does this firm rank?

material: A client is considering an acquisition of a competitor. During your due diligence, you discover that the target company's revenue projections appear overly optimistic compared to industry benchmarks.
text: How would you raise this concern with the deal team and the client?
Tell me about a time you built a financial model or performed a detailed quantitative analysis. What was the result?
Where do you see yourself in five years within the finance industry?
What industry or sector are you most interested in covering, and why?
Describe a situation where you had to quickly learn a new financial concept or tool to complete an assignment.
Tell me about a leadership experience where you had to motivate a team to meet a tight deadline.

material: You are working on a live deal. At 2 AM, you discover an error in the financial model that could materially change the valuation range presented to the client. The pitch is at 8 AM.
text: Walk me through exactly what you would do in this situation.
What is the most important quality you would bring to our analyst class?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a supervisor. How did you handle it?
How do you stay informed about financial markets and current events?
If you had to pitch a stock right now, which company would you choose and why?

material: You are a first-year analyst. A Vice President asks you to make a change to a presentation that you believe contains misleading information, though it is not technically false.
text: How would you handle this situation?
Tell me about a time when your integrity was tested.
